title: Parsanol 0.3.0: Captures, Scopes, and Dynamic Atoms
summary: Major release adding capture extraction, isolated scopes, and context-sensitive parsing.

content.text: Parsanol 0.3.0 introduces three powerful new features for extracting and managing parsed data: **Capture Atoms**, **Scope Atoms**, and **Dynamic Atoms**.
Extract named values from your input, like named groups in regular expressions but integrated into the parsing grammar.
Create isolated capture contexts for nested parsing. Captures made inside a scope are discarded when the scope exits.
Runtime-determined parsing via callbacks. Enable context-sensitive parsing.

items: Streaming parser capture persistence across chunks

title: Parsanol Ruby 1.2.2: FFI Captures, Scopes, and Dynamic Atoms
summary: Ruby bindings now support native capture extraction, isolated scopes, and context-sensitive parsing via FFI callbacks.

content.text: Parsanol Ruby 1.2.2 brings full FFI support for the new features introduced in parsanol-rs 0.3.0. Ruby code can now use capture atoms, scope atoms, and dynamic atoms with native performance.

require 'parsanol'

parser = str('hello').capture(:greeting)
result = parser.parse('hello world')
puts result[:greeting].to_s  # => "hello"

# Type-driven parsing with FFI callbacks
parser = match('[a-z]+').capture(:type) >> str(':') >>
        dynamic { |ctx|
          case ctx[:type].to_s
          when 'int' then match('\d+')
          when 'str' then match('[a-z]+')
          else match('[a-z]+')
          end
        }

result = parser.parse('int:42')
puts result[:type].to_s  # => "int"
The StreamingBuilder module provides maximum performance by eliminating intermediate AST construction. Include `Parsanol::BuilderCallbacks` in your class to receive parse events directly.

items: FFI bindings for capture, scope, and dynamic atoms
Fixed native extension loading for precompiled gems

title: Parsanol 0.2.0: Streaming Parser and Benchmarks
summary: Added streaming parser for large files, comprehensive benchmarks, and improved documentation.

content.text: Parsanol 0.2.0 brings streaming parser support, comprehensive benchmarking tools, and significant documentation improvements.
Parse large files without loading them into memory. The streaming parser processes input in configurable chunks with a sliding window for backtracking support.
New comprehensive benchmark suite comparing all backends across different grammar types.

# All examples
cargo bench --bench all-examples

# Backend comparison
cargo bench --bench backend-comparison

items: Improved error messages with source context

title: Parsanol 0.1.0: Initial Release
summary: First public release of Parsanol, a modern parser combinator library for Rust and Ruby.

content.text: Parsanol is a modern parser combinator library that combines the elegance of PEG parsing with the performance of Rust. It provides multiple parsing backends, rich error reporting, and seamless Ruby bindings.
Five integration approaches from pure Ruby to ZeroCopy+Slice for maximum flexibility and performance. Parslet-compatible API makes migration straightforward.

rows: Return Slice objects for lazy extraction

content.text: 30+ complete examples included for real-world formats:

items: **Data formats**: JSON, CSV, XML, TOML, YAML

title: Parsanol Ruby 1.1.0: Slice Support and Performance
summary: Position information now returned by default with Slice objects, plus ZeroCopy API and parallel batch parsing.

content.text: Parsanol Ruby 1.1.0 introduces Slice objects for position tracking, ZeroCopy API for maximum performance, and parallel batch parsing capabilities.
All parse methods now return `Parsanol::Slice` objects by default, providing offset, length, and line/column information without performance overhead.

result = parser.parse('hello world')
puts result.offset            # => 0
puts result.length           # => 5
puts result.line_and_column  # => [1, 1]
puts result.extract_from(input)  # => "hello"
Direct FFI object construction for maximum performance. Bypass Ruby object creation entirely for speed-critical paths.

# ZeroCopy mode - fastest option
result = Parsanol::Native.parse_to_objects(grammar, input)
# Returns Ruby objects directly from Rust, no intermediate AST
New `Parsanol::Parallel` module for multi-core batch processing. Parse thousands of files concurrently with automatic workload distribution.

files = Dir.glob('data/*.json')
results = Parsanol::Parallel.parse_files(parser, files, threads: 4)

items: Native extension ~20x faster than pure Ruby

title: Parsanol Ruby 1.0.2: Release Infrastructure
summary: Improved release workflow with trusted publishing for secure gem distribution.

content.text: Parsanol Ruby 1.0.2 improves the release infrastructure with trusted publishing to RubyGems, ensuring secure and reliable gem distribution.

items: Trusted publishing to RubyGems (no API keys needed)
Better error handling for gem pushes

title: Parsanol Ruby 1.0.0: Initial Release
summary: First release of Parsanol Ruby, a high-performance PEG parser library with Rust native extension.

content.text: Parsanol Ruby 1.0.0 is the initial release, providing a Parslet-compatible API with optional Rust native extension for 17-42x faster parsing.

items: **Great error reporting** with source location tracking

content.text:
# Gemfile
gem 'parsanol', '~> 1.0'

# Or install directly
gem install parsanol
